---
title: Dataset
---

## About the Dataset

This project uses data from the [General Social Survey (GSS)](https://gss.norc.org/), a long-running survey that collects information about people in the United States. It includes topics like education, opinions, behaviors, and social trends.

The GSS has been conducted since 1972 and is used by researchers to study how society changes over time.

## The Project Dataset

The dataset you are using is a subset of the full GSS. It has been selected and prepared to make it easier to explore in this project.

## What Was Changed?

Before you received the dataset, a few adjustments were made:

- A smaller set of relevant variables was selected  
- Some variables were renamed for clarity  
- A new variable, `age_group`, was created  
- The variable `wwwhr` was converted to numeric values  
- The dataset was limited to respondents age 51 and under  
- Some missing or unclear responses were cleaned or left as-is  

This means you are working with a prepared dataset, not the raw original data.

## How the Data Is Organized

The dataset is structured to make analysis easier:

- Each row represents one person  
- Each column represents one variable  
- Each cell contains a single value  

The data has been simplified so you can focus on:

- Asking questions  
- Exploring patterns  
- Interpreting results  

while still working with real-world data.
